question: You need to scan ports 80, 99, and 443 on the network 192.168.1.0/24 while ensuring a very slow, stealthy scan timing to avoid IDS detection. What command do you use?

You are scanning a target that is known to block all ICMP packets, and you want to ensure Nmap skips host discovery and proceeds directly to port scanning. What flag do you use?
If you are using Termux on Android to install Nmap, what is the exact command you would run in the terminal?
When scanning as a root user, Nmap sends four specific packets for host discovery. Besides the TCP SYN on 443, TCP ACK on 80, and ICMP timestamp request, what is the fourth packet type?
You want to save the output of your scan to a file named 'results.txt' in normal format. What flag do you append to your command?
You suspect a firewall is filtering packets and want to perform a TCP SYN scan (half-open scan) on a target. What flag do you use?
What is the specific Nmap flag used to perform service version detection to identify what application is running on the open ports?
To perform an OS detection scan on a target IP address, what flag must you use?
If you want to use the most aggressive timing template to complete a scan as quickly as possible, which flag do you use?
What is the specific Nmap flag to scan all 65535 TCP ports on a target?
When you perform a TCP SYN scan, if the port is closed, what packet does the target send back to Nmap?
What command-line flag is used in Nmap to provide a verbose output during the scanning process?
If you want to perform a UDP scan instead of the default TCP scan, what flag do you use?
You want to scan a specific list of IP addresses contained within a text file named 'targets.txt'. What flag do you use?
In the context of Nmap, what is the technical name for the scan type that sends a TCP packet with the FIN flag set to determine port status?
What is the specific Nmap flag used to perform a 'script scan' using the Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE) with default categories?
Which Nmap flag allows you to randomize the order in which the hosts are scanned to further evade detection?
What is the Nmap flag used to specify a custom source port for the outgoing scan packets?
If you want to output the scan results in XML format for integration with other security tools, which flag do you use?
